rationale: Postcondition requires concrete `HashMap<V>` equality on `post1_self_.{receive,send}_state.epmap == post2_self_...epmap`. The only constraints from `SingleDelivery::send_single_message` are on abstract views (`pre_self_@`, `post1_self_@`, `post2_self_@`). `HashMap<V>` here wraps `std::collections::HashMap` with `uninterp spec fn view(self) -> Map<...>`, so abstract-view equality cannot be lifted to concrete `HashMap` equality. The elaborate proof block discharges everything that is provable (Some/None alignment across r1/r2, abstract `post1@ == post2@`, equality of underlying `Map<AbstractEndPoint, _>` views via `=~=`/`map_values`), but no axiom ties two concrete HashMaps returned by independent external_body calls to each other. Determinism check is intrinsically over-strict for this externally implemented function.
